mechanism_narrative: Persistent high unemployment signals weak consumer demand and business uncertainty, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns. A sustained unemployment rate above a certain threshold indicates a struggling economy that will weigh on market sentiment.
regime_hint: post-recessionary recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is above 7.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and forward returns is consistently positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth indicates strong consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as earnings expectations adjust to improved demand conditions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in periods where RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annualized, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals that monetary policy is too accommodative relative to price pressures. This imbalance can lead to future aggressive tightening and market instability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive or flat during periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the interaction term shows no statistically significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts (HOUST) after a downturn indicates renewed confidence in the economy and future demand. This acts as a leading indicator for broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 returns in the very short term as sentiment improves.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average growth > 10% after a period of decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant following a 10% surge in HOUST after a decline, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing manufacturing activity and business investment. This signals a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is consistently negative or zero over any 5-year rolling window, or the model's R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust job creation and a strengthening labor market. This leads to increased consumer spending and improved corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS shows strong growth, or the signal's predictive accuracy is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is weak or declining while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates a stagflationary environment. Economic activity contracts but prices rise, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced corporate profits and consumer purchasing power.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive during periods of low GDP growth and high inflation, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL shows no significant negative impact on returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening. This typically aims to curb inflation but also slows economic activity and increases borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the short term as markets react to tighter conditions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a rapid FEDFUNDS hike, or the Rank IC is below 0.01 during tightening cycles over any 2-year rolling window.
